I also had the opportunity to sit down with Gloria Mark, a psychologist at the University of California, Irvine, who has spent the last 30 years studying how people interact with digital techno
articles.content: This week IвҖҷve been at SXSW London . ThereвҖҷs been music, film, and a lotвҖ”and I mean a lot вҖ”of talk about AI. I also had the opportunity to sit down with Gloria Mark, a psychologist at the University of California, Irvine, who has spent the last 30 years studying how people interact with digital technologies. Early in her career, the biggest concerns were the potential impacts of internet and email use on our brains. We may laugh those concerns off today, but itвҖҷs true that as the technologies be
articles.author: Jessica Hamzelou

articles.title: The Meta hack shows thereвҖҷs more to AI security than Mythos
articles.description: On June 5, 404 Media reported that attackers had been using MetaвҖҷs AI customer support agent to steal Instagram accounts. Their approach was simple: They asked the agent to link the accounts to email addresses that they controlled, and the agent complied. One attacker broke into the dormant Obama Wh
articles.content: On June 5, 404 Media reported that attackers had been using MetaвҖҷs AI customer support agent to steal Instagram accounts. Their approach was simple: They asked the agent to link the accounts to email addresses that they controlled, and the agent complied. One attacker broke into the dormant Obama White House account and made pro-Iran posts; others took over accounts with valuable, single-word handles, possibly in order to sell them. AI cybersecurity concerns are nothing new. Since Anthropic anno
articles.author: Grace Huckins

This is because organizations and businesses are using data to make decisions, solve problems, and develop actionable insights. Often, the available datasets are messy. Programs such as Microsoft Excel exist to transform
articles.content: Every once in a while, you will hear the catchy phrase "data is the new gold." This is because organizations and businesses are using data to make decisions, solve problems, and develop actionable insights. Often, the available datasets are messy. Programs such as Microsoft Excel exist to transform the raw, messy data into meaningful insights that can be used to make data-driven decisions. As a beginner learning data analytics, I have encountered Excel as one of the tools required for my day-to-
articles.author: Ian Githu

articles.content: Compliance Just Got Real: Navigating the Post-Honeymoon Era of AI The artificial intelligence landscape just experienced its "Napster moment." The era of unchecked, "move fast and break things" AI deployment is unequivocally over. Regulators in the nascent Global AI Oversight Board (GAIOB) have issued a staggering вӮ¬500 million fine against CognitoSync Labs. Their offense? Demonstrable algorithmic bias in a financial lending platform, coupled with a severe lack of explainability under the new Glo

articles.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
articles.description: Seller of the Sound Blaster Katana V2X doesn't consider the behavior a vulnerability.
articles.content: Operating system makers take many steps to prevent their wares from accepting commands from remote devices. The safeguards, designed to thwart malicious attacks, typically require hackers to jump through all kinds of hoops to bypass the measures. But what if remote code execution were as simple as being within Bluetooth range of a speaker connected to the targeted device? It turns out it can, at least when the speaker is a Sound Blaster Katana V2X sold by Singapore-based Creative Technologies. T
articles.author: Dan Goodin

articles.title: GitHub Universe is back: All together now, in the agentic era
articles.description: GitHub Universe is back: returning to the historic Fort Mason Center in San Francisco on October 28вҖ“29, 2026. The post GitHub Universe is back: All together now, in the agentic era appeared first on The GitHub Blog .
articles.content: If you’ve been following all the AI agent conversations and wondering what’s useful versus what’s just noise, you’re not alone. There are ideas everywhere. What’s challenging is finding the time and a practical path from cool demos to workflows that make your day easier. GitHub Universe bridges that gap. Universe is our flagship event for developers and the teams who support them—builders, maintainers, security practitioners, technical leaders, and partners&md
articles.author: Natalie Guevara

articles.description: Ryan welcomes back Tanya Janca, now part of the OWASP Top 10 team, to discuss what changed in the latest OWASP Top 10 release, how the list shifted from вҖңoutdated componentsвҖқ to a broader software supply chain focus, and why they added memory safety and vibe-coding as awareness items.вҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖҢ вҖҚ вҖӢвҖҚвҖӢвҖҚвҖҢвҖҚ

articles.title: Kabuto Park captures the fleeting joy of summer vacation
articles.description: There are a lot of games that remind me of summer - hot days in the backseat with a copy of Dragon Warrior III, cooling off in the basement while grinding Gran Turismo races - but there aren't a lot of games that are actually about summer. That's part of what makes Kabuto Park so […]
articles.content: There are a lot of games that remind me of summer - hot days in the backseat with a copy of Dragon Warrior III , cooling off in the basement while grinding Gran Turismo races - but there aren't a lot of games that are actually about summer. That's part of what makes Kabuto Park so charming. It's a game that manages to not only capture the fleeting moments of a childhood summer, but also cram a Pokémon -style adventure into a game that lasts only a few hours. Kabuto Park actually launched
articles.author: Andrew Webster

articles.title: Meta made its own AI-generated clickbait news feed
articles.description: Facebook has long been filled with feeds of clickbait articles. Now, Meta is making its own clickbait articles with AI. The standalone Meta AI app now has a "For You" section that populates a list of clickbait-style stories for you to read. But the topics, images, and text are all AI-generated - and
articles.content: An AI-generated image of the royal family featuring two Queen Elizabeth IIs. | Image: Meta AI Facebook has long been filled with feeds of clickbait articles. Now, Meta is making its own clickbait articles with AI. The standalone Meta AI app now has a "For You" section that populates a list of clickbait-style stories for you to read. But the topics, images, and text are all AI-generated - and as questionable as you'd expect from AI-created works. The Meta AI app first launched in April 2025 with
articles.author: Robert Hart

articles.title: Here comes new Siri again
articles.description: Apple has been on its back foot, AI-wise, for the past few years. But in a strange way, playing from behind might not be such a bad move. At WWDC on Monday, Apple appears to be getting ready to reintroduce us to the new Siri. Again. As a reminder, we met the new Siri in […]
articles.content: Our first glimpse of the new AI Siri came all the way back at WWDC 2024. Apple has been on its back foot , AI-wise, for the past few years. But in a strange way, playing from behind might not be such a bad move. At WWDC on Monday, Apple appears to be getting ready to reintroduce us to the new Siri . Again. As a reminder, we met the new Siri in 2024 when Apple "launched" Apple Intelligence. Siri came with a new glowing border, different voice options, and the ability to punt questions to ChatGPT.
articles.author: Allison Johnson

If you visit https://www.gjb.au/fomop you wi
articles.content: Hi folks. I am not sure if this is the right thread for this question... I have thos domain: https://www.gjb.au On here I have multiple website hosted in sperate top level folders. https://www.gjb.au/fomop And... https://www.gjb.au/millhouse Among others. If you visit https://www.gjb.au/fomop you will notice favicon.ico is working fine. It is located at https://www.gjb.au/fomop/favicon.ico If you visit https://www.gjb.au/millhouse you will notice that favicon.ico is not working despite that file

articles.content: This is today’s edition of The Download , our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The Meta hack shows thereвҖҷs more to AI security than Mythos On Monday, reports emerged that attackers had used MetaвҖҷs AI customer support agent to steal Instagram accounts. articles.description: The reactor, from a startup called Antares, isn't ready to generate power yet.
articles.content: Just over a year ago, the Trump Administration issued an executive order meant to accelerate the development of nuclear power in the US. While an entire startup ecosystem has developed around the use of differentвҖ”and typically smallerвҖ”reactor designs, only one of them has been fully licensed so far, and there are no plans to actually build any instances of that design. The executive order directed the Department of Energy to have three different reactor designs reach criticality in a bit over a

articles.title: Some ancient microbes frozen with Г–tzi the Iceman are still growing
articles.description: WhatвҖҷs the difference between a person, an artifact, and an ecosystem?
articles.content: Г–tzi the Iceman, EuropeвҖҷs most famous mummy, is crawling with microbes, some long dead, some still eking out a living after thousands of years, and some very modern. After he died in the Г–tztal Alps, the Copper Age man now known as Г–tzi lay alone and forgotten for 5,300 years, until a group of hikers stumbled on his freeze-dried remains in 1991. Since then, heвҖҷs received a lot of attention from scientists, who have sequenced his DNA , pored over his last meal and the remains of his gut microbes,

articles.title: Baby botulism outbreak: FDA still doesn't know causeвҖ”or how to prevent it
articles.description: In the end, the three companies involved all point the finger at each other.
articles.content: The Food and Drug Administration this week posted what critics call an "underwhelming" epilogue to the devastating outbreak of botulism in babies , which was linked to spore-contaminated formula made by ByHeart . Despite clear tracking of the contamination, the regulator still doesn't know how the bacteria arrived in the formulaвҖ”or how to prevent it from happening again. "The FDA's investigation into the root cause is ongoing with a focus on ingredients," the agency reported. In the void, three
articles.author: Beth Mole
